title: The short movies would you like them to be canon or not
display_title: The short movies would you like them to be canon or not
wikitext:
[]: The short movies - would you like them to be canon or not?             Quote Post by tzigi » Tue Jan 13, 2015 11:37 am The title of this thread is pretty much self-explanatory. As of now there are two (and a third one that could appear shortly) short Eva-related movies released between 3.0 and 3.0+1.0/Final/Whatever-you-want-to-call-the-last-NME-movie: 1. Peaceful Times 2. Until You Come to me (3. the movie with a black CG Evangelion ) Those that we've seen so far look as though they could quite nicely fit after the end of 3.0. The story SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Jan 13, 2015 12:21 pm I presume that the scenes seen in the two shorts arise from the (?incomplete) preparation of further scenes that might for a time have been planned to become part of Q but in the end did not. Since these storyboards and scenes depicting the journey of the pilots have been allowed out, I further presume that they will not be shown in the next film, because that would repeat what we've already seen now - and so the film will start at the end of their journey or some time after. I imagine that, for what they're worth (not very much, honestly), they can be considered canon until we learn otherwise. I expect many people to take the opposite view, that as they are not part of the declared sequence of films, they are not canon. At this stage it really is just a personal choice.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Tue Jan 13, 2015 2:55 pm As much as I love cool ways of multimedia-marketing, I believe that the four movies should be able to stand on their own, without the need to resort to other media. So if this walking part stays the only significant part for the development of those characters because Final sinks into an orgy of explosions, that would be lame.

[]:             Quote Post by tzigi » Tue Jan 13, 2015 3:20 pm pwhodges wrote: I imagine that, for what they're worth (not very much, honestly), they can be considered canon until we learn otherwise. I expect many people to take the opposite view, that as they are not part of the declared sequence of films, they are not canon. At this stage it really is just a personal choice.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Jan 13, 2015 3:47 pm I doubt that they contain anything that materially affects how the story will go.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Jan 13, 2015 5:00 pm They're cute. Sure you can count them as canon. But as long as you don't need to view them to get any pertinent information that's in the films then they're all A-Ok in my book. The nadir of "multi-media" storytelling still has to be the Matrix sequels. There were whole character archs that got cut out of the films & tossed into video games & what not. So if you were like me & you didn't want to spend 20 hours on a badly made video game tie in to a sequel that character in the film you're supposed to care for is just a nameless person.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Jan 13, 2015 5:54 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: There were whole character archs that got cut out of the films & tossed into video games & what not.

[]:             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Tue Jan 13, 2015 7:47 pm With the first two, I do believe in some respect they will end up being canon. Even if the Children waking through the city is altered it will still remain that they walked through the ruined city. If in 4.0 somethings from Untill You Come to Me is altered then that whole short film would've just been teasing at some of the things to come. I believe that Peaceful times was teasing stuff that was quite obviously still in early production, but that doesn't mean the concepts presents are null and void, they could just be presented in the final film a completely diffrent way. Also I imagine they wouldn't use a short film to fill in a gap between the third and last movie, so things seen in Untill You Come to Me will likely be shown in 4.0 even if changed. The short film I believe was suppose to be vague and be a hyper for NTE content to come. Now with the Black Evangelion, so we see it in the promo. I was the first to preach that it will likely be teaser that all of us want to see to the final movie. I still stand by this, and to conclude with the fact that it hasn't been released yet that Anno is saving it. Why? Untill You Come to Me was the last short of 2014 and 7 out of 30. Anno and Khara have repeatedly said after the Expo they will work to finish Final. What better way to promote that then to have the last short be a teaser for Final? Granted I could also be completely wrong about all of this, and many of you are likely going to tell me why, but hey that's my belief and I don't see any problems with it. I actually want to hear what people think about this.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Tue Jan 13, 2015 9:29 pm I think that Until You Come to Me is canon, or is at least foreshadowing of some kind. Not sure about the other two though.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Tue Jan 13, 2015 10:56 pm I think the intention by Khara is that UYCTM is canon and, by extension, PT, since they don't contradict one another and follow logically from the point where Q ended. I haven't seen whatever clip the Black Evangelion was in. Was that just in the trailer for the entire expo along with clips for everything else? 'Cause that'd be something to look forward to. What I would really like to know is whether 2.0's trailer for Q contains canon events during the timeskip...because there hasn't been a statement from the staff one way or another on this. I guess the only way for us to get the answer at this point is by seeing Final.

[]:             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Tue Jan 13, 2015 11:03 pm Guy Nacks wrote: I think the intention by Khara is that UYCTM is canon and, by extension, PT, since they don't contradict one another and follow logically from the point where Q ended. I haven't seen whatever clip the Black Evangelion was in. Was that just in the trailer for the entire expo along with clips for everything else? 'Cause that'd be something to look forward to. What I would really like to know is whether 2.0's trailer for Q contains canon events during the timeskip...because there hasn't been a statement from the staff one way or another on this. I guess the only way for us to get the answer at this point is by seeing Final.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Jan 13, 2015 11:13 pm Guy Nacks wrote: What I would really like to know is whether 2.0's trailer for Q contains canon events during the timeskip...because there hasn't been a statement from the staff one way or another on this. I guess the only way for us to get the answer at this point is by seeing Final.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Tue Jan 13, 2015 11:16 pm Sicarius VI wrote: http://animatorexpo.com/promotional/ There you go for the video with the Black Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Tue Jan 13, 2015 11:28 pm Guy Nacks wrote: There's probably still a fair bit of material that we haven't seen, if there are supposed to be 30 different shorts in total. Also, there isn't enough information one way or the other to make a judgement of any sort on Black Evangelion yet. For all we know, it could just be concept CGI.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Jan 14, 2015 7:10 am The only thing that keeps be from seeing UYCTM as canon to NTE is the Asuka montage. That, and anytime multiple authors are at work helping a project (Anno had no creative input on UYCTM), I tend to hold the canonic nature of the work loosely. There’s no guarantee that Tatsuya Kushida was concerned about telling an Eva story with his short film, or that Hideki Anno is concerned with recognizing UYCTM while telling his Eva Shin story. So emphatically stating “Yes,” or “No,” is a little dangerous within terms of narrative coherency. (My leaning is “No,” though, due to the above mentioned montage.) But even then, I might still fancy watching UYCTM before every viewing of Eva Shin unless Anno proves that it’s not a good idea to do so.

[]:             Quote Post by vito » Wed Jan 14, 2015 10:07 am Where did they state, that Anno had no creative input on UYCTM, given that he's one of the three people credited for the layouts. Was it the Nico broadcast?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Jan 14, 2015 11:09 am vito wrote: Where did they state, that Anno had no creative input on UYCTM, given that he's one of the three people credited for the layouts. Was it the Nico broadcast?

[]:             Quote Post by Jäeger » Wed Jan 14, 2015 12:50 pm They are just animation exercises, pire teasing, so no. Accepting them as canon has the same sense as accepting Girlfriend of steel as canonical ,and we're talking about a game where the series staff was implied and it's new character is present in artbooks (Even Sadamoto's) and the only canon inmigrant in the franchise. The 3.0 preview of 2.0 is another story.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Jan 14, 2015 2:08 pm Well, unless there's actually a WILLE VTOL waiting for them on the other side of that dune where Q ended, they must had walked a fair bit in the ruined lands to reach a safe extraction zone, so personally I take those short films as a representation of how it was, I seriously doubt that if something happens besides the three walking with Shinji depressed, Asuka pissed off and Rei Q being Rei Q, it'll appear in those short films instead of Shin Eva. Now maybe the important moments of their trip will be shown in Shin Eva (whatever important conversation or anything that count as character development), leaving all the boring hiking to the short movies, or maybe nothing interesting will happen during that trip, in which case it'll all be shown in the short films to free screen time, and the movie will begin with the trio already somewhere else. (Wunder, WILLE base, Lilin settlement...)

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Wed Jan 14, 2015 3:22 pm Definitely non canon.
